offshore 和 onshore 是英文中用来描述地理位置关系的两个词汇。它们通常用来区分一个实体位于国内还是海外。 offshore 的概念主要表示在海外或离岸的地区。例如，当留学澳大利亚的学生毕业后，选择留在澳大利亚并申请移民，我们称其为 onshore application。这里的 onshore 指的是在澳大利亚国内进行的 ...

Bonjour French friends,
Hereby I share my latest project: a 500mm long Mini Mono, based on John Finch Wildthing.
I share the drawing for free, and you can download it here:
https://drive.google.com/file/d/18h5v1BeNiVeqVx38g8DY5kYUVbUtM2Ao/view?usp=drivesdk
I started with sticking the drawing parts to the wood, for cutting the frames. You can use 2-3mm plywood:
I added 1mm thick birch plywood for the plating. No fibreglass, just glue:
Next to it's big brother, which I badly crashed previous season:
I added the servo tray and the motor. I start with a 28mm 2600kV Roxxy inrunner, which I still had:
I added a cheap rudder, with water cooling pickup. Basically, all parts I used are very cheap:
I made my own trim tabs and turn fins:
I made the cockpit from hard foam, laminated with 2 layers of glass weave:
Ready to run! It weighs just 873 grams including a 3S 3000mAh lipo:
Maiden run! It runs so great, I'm in love
😊
. It handles very good. It can take corners at full speed. The top speed is 53km/h with a 435 brass prop.
I made a 3-part video build series of this little boat. Here's the playlist, if you're interested:
RC Mini Mono build series:
My sons will enjoy this little boat now. Soon enough, I will start upgrading it
😎
. Adding a Rocket 2948 3450kV motor at first, running on 3S, and maybe try 4S. I would love to get 80km/h with this Mini Mono!
5

I'm now making a carbon hull. I made my own carbon plate from 2x 200gr/m2 weave. Laminated with 45 degrees angle between the weave strands.
The transom is still 3mm ply, so I can use inserts to install the hardware. I laminated the seams with a strip of weave, after gluing the hull together. It's very strong, and so far just 88 grams for this 50cm hull.

je vien de recevoir un nouveau moteur brushless pour mon bateau:

# Axial Diameter: 3.175mm
# Shaft diameter: 3.175mm
# Shaft Length: 12 mm
# motor mount position: 16 mm and 19mm, M3
je vien de metre ce trontroleur:
80A Brushless Motor Speed Controller RC ESC Parts
Features
* Constant Current: 80A
* Sustaining Voltage: (Li-ion/Li-poly: 7.4 - 22.2v) / (NiMh/Nicd: 6 - 21.6v)
* Max Current: 90A
* Low-Voltage Cutoff: Auto detect and set
* Size: 66.5mm x 38mm x 13.5mm
* Weight:50g
Specifications:
* High rate PWM
* Low resistence
* User programmable brake
* Low voltage auto setting based on battery
* Throttle range self-adjusting
* Soft start ramp up
* Auto motor cutoff with reset
* Runs motor in forward or reverse
* Safe power arming program ensures motor will not run accidentally after turn on
* Low torque start
* Auto shut down when signal is lost
100% Brand New
1 x MYSTERY 80A Speed Controller ESC

14.8v 5000mAh 15C 4S Lipo neuf
marque WinForce.

* Size :176mm x 48mm x 27mm

* marque EC-POWER
* de très très bonne qualité
* 1 x 14.8v 5000mAh 4-cell 15C Li-Po Battery

Une bonne nouvelle : ta transmission est semi immergée
Une mauvaise : elle n'est pas faite pour supporter beaucoup de puissance. Il ne faut pas investir trop dans cette coque. Tu peux essayer le config dont je t'ai parlée (K48 et moteur feigao de 1500 -1700 de kv) mais n'attends pas de miracles.
Après mon conseil serait de changer de coque. En sachant que le kv de 1500 à 1700 n'est pas adapté à du mono 2 qui est la coque standard de vitesse.
A toi de voir si tu veux investir dans cette coque (au maximum 50 km/h) ou passer sur un mono 2 pour aller vers 70 - 80 km/h.
En tout cas, n'utilise pas le moteur que tu as. Même à vide, la transmission par cardan n'y resistera pas.

J'iamgine que tu veux faire un bateau de vitesse. Oublions donc la coque, il faudra en acheter une nouvelle. Tu peux garder ta radio AM au debut si les deux voies sont proportionnelles, mais il faudra assez vite une radio FM.
Ensuite le moteur et les accus ne vont pas ensemble. Ton moteur est pour un mono 1 et l'accus est celui d'un mono 2. Le contro convient pour les deux (un peu juste pour le mono 2 dans une version un peu sportive ou si les caracteristiques du constructeur sont un peu optimistes.
Il faut choisir soit monter un mono 1 avec ton moteur et des accus 2S (en verifiant que ton controleur fonctionne en 2S) soit un mono 2 avec tes accus 4S.
Hors frais de port, tu peux trouver des moteurs à 50 ?. Le hardware varie entre 40 et 120 ? selon la qualité.
Une coque peut démarrer à 50 ? sur ebay et monter à 150 voir 200 chez un fabriquant de qualité
Si tu n'as pas de pb de budget, le mono 2 est plus gros, va plus vite et passe mieux les vagues mais c'est un peu plus cher.
Bonne réflexion !
A+
Pierre
PS : tu peux aussi choisr un acatamaran ou un hydro.

						 From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia 
					 
					    
					
					
					  
   Using hydrogen to decarbonize more sectors 
     Hydrogen has the most potential to reduce  greenhouse gas emissions  when used in chemical production, refineries, international shipping, and  steelmaking    [ 1 ]     
 The  hydrogen economy  is a term for the role  hydrogen  as an energy carrier to complement electricity as part of a long-term option to reduce emissions of  greenhouse gases . The aim is to reduce emissions where cheaper and more energy-efficient clean solutions are not available.   [ 2 ]     : 1    In this context,  hydrogen economy  encompasses the production of hydrogen and the use of hydrogen in ways that contribute to  phasing-out fossil fuels  and limiting  climate change .
  Hydrogen can be produced by several means. Most hydrogen produced today is  gray hydrogen , made from  natural gas  through  steam methane reforming  (SMR). This process accounted for 1.8% of global greenhouse gas emissions in 2021.   [ 3 ]     Low-carbon hydrogen , which is made using SMR with  carbon capture and storage  (  blue hydrogen  ), or through electrolysis of water using renewable power (  green hydrogen  ), accounted for less than 1% of production.   [ 4 ]    Of the 100 million tonnes   [ 5 ]    of hydrogen produced in 2021, 43% was used in oil refining and 57% in  industry, principally in the manufacture of  ammonia  for fertilizers, and  methanol .   [ 6 ]     : 18, 22, 29   
  To  limit global warming , it is generally envisaged that the future hydrogen economy replace  gray hydrogen with low-carbon hydrogen. As of 2024 it is unclear when enough low-carbon hydrogen could be produced to phase-out all the gray hydrogen.   [ 7 ]    The future end-uses are likely in heavy industry (e.g. high-temperature processes alongside electricity, feedstock for production of  green ammonia  and  organic chemicals , as alternative to coal-derived  coke  for  steelmaking ), long-haul transport (e.g. shipping, and to a lesser extent  hydrogen-powered aircraft  and heavy goods vehicles), and long-term energy storage.   [ 8 ]      [ 9 ]    Other applications, such as light duty vehicles and heating in buildings, are no longer part of the future hydrogen economy, primarily for economic and environmental reasons.   [ 10 ]      [ 11 ]    Hydrogen is challenging to store, to transport in pipelines, and to use.   [ 12 ]    It presents  safety  concerns since it is highly explosive, and it is inefficient compared to direct  use of electricity . Since relatively small amounts of low-carbon hydrogen are available, climate benefits can be maximized by using it in harder-to-decarbonize applications.   [ 11 ]   
  As of 2023  [update]   there are no real alternatives to hydrogen for several chemical processes in which it is currently used, such as  ammonia production  for  fertilizer .   [ 13 ]    The cost of low- and zero-carbon hydrogen is likely to influence the degree to which it will be used in chemical feedstocks, long haul aviation and shipping, and long-term energy storage. Production costs of low- and zero-carbon hydrogen are evolving.   [ 14 ]    Future costs may be influenced by  carbon taxes , the geography and geopolitics of energy, energy prices, technology choices, and their raw material requirements.   [ 15 ]    The U.S. Department of Energy's Hydrogen Hotshot Initiative seeks to reduce the cost of green hydrogen drop to $1 a kilogram by 2031,   [ 16 ]    though the cost of electrolyzers rose 50% between 2021 and 2024.   [ 17 ]   
 
 
  History and objectives   [   edit   ]   
  Origins   [   edit   ]   
 The concept of a society that uses hydrogen as the primary means of energy storage was theorized by geneticist  J. B. S. Haldane  in 1923. Anticipating the exhaustion of Britain's coal reserves for power generation, Haldane proposed a network of wind turbines to produce hydrogen and oxygen for long-term energy storage through  electrolysis , to help address renewable power's  variable output .   [ 18 ]    The term "hydrogen economy" itself was coined by  John Bockris  during a talk he gave in 1970 at  General Motors  (GM) Technical Center.   [ 19 ]    Bockris viewed it as an economy in which hydrogen, underpinned by  nuclear  and  solar  power, would help address growing concern about fossil fuel depletion and environmental pollution, by serving as  energy carrier  for end-uses in which  electrification  was not suitable.   [ 2 ]      [ 20 ]   
  A hydrogen economy was proposed by the  University of Michigan  to solve some of the negative effects of using  hydrocarbon  fuels where the carbon is released to the atmosphere (as carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, unburnt hydrocarbons, etc.). Modern interest in the hydrogen economy can generally be traced to a 1970 technical report by  Lawrence W. Jones  of the University of Michigan,   [ 21 ]    in which he echoed Bockris' dual rationale of addressing energy security and environmental challenges. Unlike Haldane and Bockris, Jones only focused on nuclear power as the energy source for electrolysis, and principally on the use of hydrogen in transport, where he regarded aviation and heavy goods transport as the top priorities.   [ 22 ]   
  In 1974  International Energy Agency  (IEA) and the  International Association for Hydrogen Energy  (IAHE) were established. These organizations had several initiatives including advocating for national strategies to increase interest and visibility of the hydrogen economy.   [ 2 ]     : 12   
 
  Later evolution   [   edit   ]   
     Technology leadership opportunities in green hydrogen value chains according to the  International Renewable Energy Agency  in 2022   [ 23 ]     : 55     
 A spike in attention for the  hydrogen economy  concept during the 2000s was repeatedly described as hype by some  critics  and proponents of alternative technologies,   [ 24 ]      [ 25 ]      [ 26 ]    and investors lost money in the  bubble .   [ 27 ]    Interest in the energy carrier resurged in the 2010s, notably with the forming of the  Hydrogen Council  in 2017. Several manufacturers released hydrogen fuel cell cars commercially, and manufacturers such as Toyota, Hyundai, and industry groups in China planned to increase numbers of the cars into the hundreds of thousands over the next decade.   [ 28 ]      [ 29 ]    However, the global scope for hydrogen's role in cars is shrinking relative to earlier expectations.   [ 30 ]      [ 31 ]     By the end of 2022, 70,200  hydrogen vehicles  had been sold worldwide,   [ 32 ]    compared with 26 million  plug-in electric vehicles .   [ 33 ]   
  Early 2020s takes on the hydrogen economy share earlier perspectives' emphasis on the complementarity of electricity and hydrogen, and the use of electrolysis as the mainstay of hydrogen production.   [ 8 ]    They focus on the need to limit  global warming  to 1.5 °C and prioritize the production, transportation and use of  green hydrogen  for heavy industry (e.g. high-temperature processes alongside electricity,   [ 34 ]    feedstock for production of  green ammonia  and organic chemicals,   [ 8 ]    as alternative to coal-derived coke for  steelmaking ),   [ 35 ]    long-haul transport (e.g. shipping, aviation and to a lesser extent heavy goods vehicles), and long-term energy storage.   [ 8 ]      [ 9 ]   
 
  National hydrogen strategies      [   edit   ]   
     Hydrogen strategy phases  
 Since 2017, several countries and regions have released a  hydrogen strategy , outlining their goals in developing hydrogen infrastructure and as a guide for private investment.   [ 36 ]    The  International Renewable Energy Agency  has proposed  national strategies  as the first pillar of policies to promote green hydrogen.   [ 36 ]      [ 37 ]   
  In 2017  Japan  published their strategy with a proposal to become the world's first "hydrogen society".   [ 38 ]    Many provinces and cities in China have established hydrogen strategies.   [ 39 ]    The  European Union  strategy, adopted   [ 40 ]    in 2021, outlines a plan to develop large scale infrastructure for hydrogen including electrolysers in collaboration with multiple trade organizations.   [ 41 ]    The US hydrogen strategy "presents a strategic framework for achieving large-scale production and use of hydrogen" over a 30 year period.   [ 42 ]    Analysis suggests that even nations reliant on exports of  natural gas  like  Qatar  could benefit from hydrogen strategies that leverage existing infrastructure, expertise, and markets.   [ 43 ]    As of 2021, 28 governments had published hydrogen strategies.   [ 44 ]    By 2024, that number reached 60.   [ 45 ]    However the actual strategies proposed are not necessarily based on climate friendly  green hydrogen . The majority of the strategies have been characterized by  scale first and clean later , meaning they add regulations to enhance the viability of green hydrogen but do not mandate it use.   [ 44 ]    Economic analysis shows few national strategies can make their 2030 goals.   [ 46 ]   
 
  Current hydrogen market   [   edit   ]   
  Hydrogen production  globally was valued at over US$155 billion in 2022 and is expected to grow over 9% annually through 2030.   [ 47 ]

source_summaries.source_title: Conception et construction perso - DiY Mini Mono
source_summaries.summary: Un utilisateur, Cozy RC, partage ses expériences et ses réalisations sur la construction d'un Mini Mono, un modèle de bateau radio-commandé. Il discute de ses choix de matériaux, de ses performances et de ses améliorations.
facts: Le Mini Mono mesure 500mm de long.
Il est construit à partir de 2-3mm de plywood.
Il est équipé d'un moteur 28mm 2600kV Roxxy inrunner.
Il est alimenté par une batterie 3S 3000mAh lipo.
Il peut atteindre une vitesse de 53km/h avec un prop de 435 brass.
Il a un poids de 873 grammes.
Il est équipé d'un rudder en bois.
Il est équipé d'un système de refroidissement à l'eau.
signals: Le Mini Mono est un modèle de bateau radio-commandé.
Il est construit à partir de matériaux de qualité.
Il est équipé d'un système de refroidissement à l'eau.
Il peut atteindre une vitesse élevée.
limitations_or_uncertainties: Le Mini Mono n'est pas conçu pour les courses de bateau.
Il est susceptible de subir des dommages si il est utilisé de manière excessive.
La durée de vie de la batterie est inconnue.
La performance du moteur peut varier en fonction des conditions de l'eau.

source_summaries.summary: Discussion sur la motorisation d'un bateau radiocommandé offshore, notamment sur le choix de l'hélice et la puissance nécessaire pour atteindre une vitesse de 50 km/h.
facts: Le bateau est un Graupner Wave Explorer Offshore
Le moteur d'origine est un SPEED 600
Le contrôleur est un 80A Brushless Motor Speed Controller
Les accus sont 14.8V 5000mAh 15C 4S Li-Po
signals: La coque n'est pas conçue pour atteindre des vitesses élevées
La transmission n'est pas faite pour supporter beaucoup de puissance
Il est recommandé de choisir une coque plus robuste pour atteindre des vitesses supérieures à 50 km/h
limitations_or_uncertainties: La puissance du moteur et la transmission ne sont pas adaptées pour atteindre des vitesses élevées
La coque n'est pas conçue pour supporter des moteurs puissants
Il est difficile de prédire les performances du bateau sans tester

editorial_note:
**Hydrogen's Offshore Leap: A Game-Changer for Decarbonization**

As the world grapples with the urgency of reducing carbon emissions, the offshore hydrogen trend is gaining momentum. This innovative approach leverages the vast ocean resources to produce hydrogen, a zero-carbon energy carrier, on a massive scale. The engineering significance lies in the potential to decarbonize industries such as shipping, power generation, and heavy industry, which are currently reliant on fossil fuels.

A recent study by the International Energy Agency (IEA) highlights the offshore hydrogen's potential to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by up to 80% in the energy sector. Moreover, the trend is gaining traction, with several countries and companies investing heavily in offshore hydrogen projects.

As this trend continues to unfold, close attention is warranted on the technical challenges associated with large-scale hydrogen production, transportation, and storage. The development of cost-effective and efficient technologies will be crucial to making offshore hydrogen a viable alternative to traditional energy sources.

The offshore hydrogen trend represents a significant shift in the energy landscape, leveraging ocean resources to produce hydrogen on a massive scale. This innovative approach has the potential to decarbonize industries reliant on fossil fuels.

**Editorial Note / Implications**
---------------------------------

**Hydrogen's Offshore Leap: A Game-Changer for Decarbonization**

The offshore hydrogen trend has the potential to revolutionize the energy sector by providing a zero-carbon energy carrier on a massive scale. Close attention is warranted on the technical challenges associated with large-scale hydrogen production, transportation, and storage.

**Open Questions**

* What are the most significant technical challenges associated with offshore hydrogen production, transportation, and storage?
